title: Tonight's beeatlas nightly will fail the JS data gate: PAGE-07 lazy-img test vs intentional eager LCP hero
description: beeatlas ed4e269f (feat(species): photo gallery, on origin/main 2026-07-18 13:59 PDT) intentionally omits loading="lazy" on the FIRST hero photo in _pages/species-detail.njk ('the first photo is the LCP candidate; the rest defer'). src/tests/build-output.data.test.ts (PAGE-07 carry-forward) still asserts EVERY on a species page has loading="lazy". Verified failing locally at clean origin/main HEAD: 1 failed / 989 passed. The nightly runs this suite as the step-4b hard gate BEFORE render/publish, so tonight's 03:00 nightly (soak night 2 — the first night exercising diff tests against a real baseline) will abort the publish and miss the healthcheck ping. SKIP_INTEGRATION_GATE does NOT bypass this gate (scoped to pytest). Fix in beeatlas before 03:00 PDT tonight, or expect the red nightly.

title: Keyed store input-addressed by file bytes missed WAL-resident writes (notes-harvest never dirtied)
description: Found live by the first production note write (st-nee, 2026-07-17): the API holds the notes store open, so committed notes live in the SQLite -wal and the main db file's bytes freeze at the last checkpoint (notes.db untouched since Jul 4 while -wal carried notes 10-12). cache.rkt input-hash addressed 'file inputs by file-sha1, so notes-harvest read 'inputs unchanged' while resolve-store-keys (DuckDB read, WAL-aware) saw the change — decision layer contradicted the observation/delta layer, and the write path published a stale harvest as 'live'. st-2k9 had modeled the slot on resolve-relation-COLUMNS (observation-only); right analog is resolve-relation (decision + observation read the same source). Fixed: keyed store input address = digest-of-pairs over its per-key digests, same coherence rule as 'dir/db-relation. Regression pinned in cache-test.rkt.
close_reason: Fixed in stelis 52c7276 (cache.rkt input-hash: digest-of-pairs over resolve-store-keys for keyed stores; regression test in cache-test.rkt; 454 pass). Deployed to maderas; the stranded bombus fervidus note re-harvested (targeted, 1 key) and verified live over HTTPS.

title: Stelis nightly: db-relation cache unsound when DuckDB is swapped (empty species_hosts)
description:
Caught by the STELIS=1 nightly verify on maderas (2026-07-17): stelis --build --all produced an EMPTY species_hosts.json where run.py produces populated (live species_hosts.json has real host genera). NOT a pre-existing data bug — a real Stelis cutover blocker.

TRACE: species_export.py:440 fails on int += NULL from dbt's species_host_plants.parquet, which materialized EMPTY (429 bytes, one NULL row). dbt-build RAN (not cached) and built occurrences.parquet (3.3MB, real) fine in the same run, but int_species_host_plants came out empty despite its 3 source tables having rows (int_ecdysis_base 46090, stg_inat__observations 13448, stg_inat__host_plant_lineage 1165). The model joins base.host_observation_id = obs.id (ecdysis host-obs -> iNat observation) then filters WHERE tle.family IS NOT NULL; under Stelis the join yields nothing -> empty.

LEADING HYPOTHESIS: warm .stelis cache from a prior verify run (built against a COPY of the DuckDB, /tmp/verify-db) caused several db-relation-producing tasks to CACHE-SKIP in the nightly (host-plant-lineage, taxon-lineage-extended, resolve-checklist-names shown as '≡ cached'). Stelis's cache decision for a db-relation OUTPUT keys on the input FILE hash only and NEVER verifies the output TABLE exists/is current in the DuckDB at DB_PATH — db-relations have no path, so the missing-output check (cache.rkt missing/env-output-paths) skips them. The nightly pulls a FRESH /tmp/beeatlas.duckdb from S3 each run (a different file/state than the cache was computed against), so a skipped db-relation task's output can be stale/absent -> a downstream dbt join silently empties. SOUNDNESS GAP: the DuckDB is external mutable state swapped between runs; Stelis content-addressing doesn't model that.

DECISIVE NEXT TEST: cold-cache re-run (rm ~/dev/stelis/.stelis; stelis --build --all) against the same DuckDB — if species_host_plants populates, the warm-cache/db-relation-skip interaction is confirmed. Candidate fixes: (a) verify a db-relation output actually exists (row-count/digest) in the current DuckDB before skipping; (b) treat DB_PATH's identity/mtime as part of every db-relation task's cache key; (c) pragmatic nightly: cold .stelis per run (full build, like run.py — boundaries always run anyway). Production is SAFE: the nightly failed before publish; only routine EXIT-trap DuckDB/taxa backups were written, no site publish.
notes: ROOT CAUSE (not the db-relation cache): the checklist task in beeatlas.rkt declared no inputs, so the planner scheduled it BEFORE ecdysis. checklist_pipeline._update_occurrences_canonical_name materializes canonical_name onto ecdysis_data.occurrences, but ecdysis_pipeline loads occurrences with write_disposition=replace (ecdysis_pipeline.py:199), which drops that column. Order was checklist(add canonical_name)->ecdysis(replace, drops it)->dbt(reads null). int_ecdysis_base.canonical_name was 0/46090 non-null -> base CTE 'WHERE canonical_name IS NOT NULL' emptied int_species_host_plants -> species_export.py:440 int+=None. run.py avoids this by linear order (...ecdysis...->checklist). FIX: added #:inputs '(ecdysis_data) to the checklist task, forcing ecdysis->checklist. VALIDATED on maderas clean cold build: species_host_plants.parquet 429->30996 bytes, species-export exit 0, species_hosts.json 541 species. (fix a / db-relation cache verify was NOT the cause.)
close_reason: Fixed by 9d3566e: added ecdysis_data as a checklist input so canonical_name materialization runs after the ecdysis replace-load. Validated on maderas clean cold build (species-export exit 0, species_hosts.json 541 species). Fix pushed and pulled to maderas.

title: Slice 2 — --explain CLI: print why each task in the plan would run or skip
description: Surface slice 1's decision records to the user. 'racket src/main.rkt --explain <target>' walks the plan (like --commands, sharing plan-suffix/--from scoping) and prints one line per task: the decision and its reason, naming changed inputs by artifact. This upgrades the current one-glyph dry-run legend (≡/≈/▶) into an actual explanation. Also decide here how 'conditional' propagation is explained: a task downstream of one that would run is stale *because* its upstream is — the explanation should say which upstream, giving a one-hop provenance chain through the plan.
acceptance_criteria: --explain <target> prints a per-task reason including the specific changed input or the upstream task that makes it conditionally stale; output scoped by --from like --commands.
close_reason: --explain prints per-task reason with named inputs/outputs; conditional skips name the upstream that causes them; pure walk-explanations unit-tested; 99 tests pass

title: Slice 1 — reasons, not booleans: cache layer returns a decision record
description: Today cache.rkt conflates its answers: input-fingerprint returns #f for three different reasons (boundary task, unresolvable input, missing file) and cache-hit? returns a bare boolean that folds together no-entry, version mismatch, fingerprint mismatch, and missing outputs. Provenance needs the distinctions. Replace the boolean core with a pure decision record — e.g. (run 'boundary), (run 'input-changed (field.csv)), (run 'recipe-changed), (run 'no-cache-entry), (run 'output-missing occurrences.db), (skip 'cached) — keeping cache-hit? as a thin wrapper so existing callers don't churn. To name WHICH input changed, the cache entry must store per-input hashes, not just the combined fingerprint (cache format is versioned; bumping CACHE-VERSION is a designed-for cache miss, not a migration). Unit tests over synthetic graphs in the model-test.rkt style.
acceptance_criteria: A pure function returns a structured why-run/why-skip decision for any task; changed inputs are named individually; existing --build/--commands behavior unchanged; raco test passes.
close_reason: cache layer returns structured decision records; per-input hashes in v2 entries name the changed input; cache-hit? kept as thin wrapper; 89 tests pass

title: Provenance, first-class: the build explains why it rebuilds (H1)
description: First Horizon 1 slice (ROADMAP.md). Horizon 0 built the machinery that decides what runs — reachability, input fingerprints, cache hits, boundary exemptions — but every decision is made and thrown away. This epic surfaces those decisions as a queryable property: 'why did this task rebuild?' / 'why is this artifact stale?'. It is the third application of the Datalog layer (after reachability and its cross-check) and pays down debugging cost for early cutoff and broader target coverage, which come next.
acceptance_criteria: For any task in a target's plan, stelis can answer why it would run or why it was skipped — naming the specific changed input, changed recipe, missing cache entry, missing output, or boundary status — both as a dry-run query and after a real --build.
close_reason: all four slices landed: decision records, --explain, Datalog why-stale chains, persisted build trace

title: Gate: verify occurrences.db reproducible (fix is beeatlas-zo7)
description: Reference/gate, not a fix. The nondeterminism is a beeatlas problem, tracked and fixed there as beeatlas-zo7 (dbt occurrences mart lacks stable ordering). This Stelis issue exists only to gate the st-d44 epic exit condition ('minimal-upstream rebuild proven reproducible'): it stays OPEN until 'racket src/main.rkt --verify --from dbt-build occurrences.db' reports DETERMINISTIC. Full diagnosis is on beeatlas-zo7. Discovered by the st-d44.6 harness.
notes:
DIAGNOSIS (localized). Ran dbt twice + generate-sqlite twice from a fixed parquet:
- occurrences.parquet: NONDETERMINISTIC across dbt runs (2c2f64a6 vs 40194a14) — ROOT CAUSE.
- occurrence_places.parquet: DETERMINISTIC (same hash) — so not all dbt output; specific to the occurrences model.
- generate-sqlite from a FIXED parquet: DETERMINISTIC (47b2b5c5 == 47b2b5c5) — the SQLite export is clean; it faithfully propagates its input.
Conclusion: nondeterminism lives entirely in beeatlas's dbt 'occurrences' mart (occurrences.sql or an int_ dependency) — classic DuckDB-parallelism row-order. Likely fix (beeatlas-side, separate session): stable ORDER BY on the final occurrences select / the COPY-to-parquet, or pin duckdb threads. occurrence_places already being stable is a hint (compare what it does differently).
VERIFIED FIXED. racket src/main.rkt --verify --from dbt-build occurrences.db now reports DETERMINISTIC — both builds byte-identical (sha1 761898069fa11d21..., 33574912 B). beeatlas-zo7's fix confirmed by the harness. This unblocks the st-d44 exit condition (minimal-upstream rebuild proven reproducible).
close_reason: Closed

title: Slice 1a — model beeatlas graph as Racket data; compute + print occurrences.db minimal upstream (plain Racket)
description: Author the full 33-node bipartite graph as Racket-native data (precise edges for occurrences.db upstream, coarse-but-honest for siblings). Compute the minimal upstream of occurrences.db in topological order and print it. No execution, no hashing. Validate: order respects run.py's hand-order; plan drops the ~15 siblings. See docs/adr/0001-build-graph-model.md.
notes:
Model core working end-to-end (src/model.rkt, src/beeatlas.rkt, src/main.rkt). Plain-Racket bipartite graph + minimal-upstream (backward reachability) + Kahn topo-sort + pruning. Verified on a real occurrences.db microcosm: gate-as-token, multi-output dbt opacity, and per-target pruning all behave correctly across 3 targets (occurrences.db, occurrences.parquet, species.json). REMAINING: broaden beeatlas.rkt from subset to full 33 nodes; add a validation test (order respects run.py edges; expected ~15 siblings prune).
DONE. Full 32-node beeatlas graph authored in src/beeatlas.rkt (precise occurrences.db-upstream edges from dbt sources.yml + run.py comments; coarse-but-honest siblings, coarse edges marked). Validation in src/plan-test.rkt (45 checks incl. order-independent topological-validity): occurrences.db plan = 21 upstream tasks, prunes exactly the 11 post-dbt steps (exports/maps/feeds/collectors/notes/dedup/topology). Honest finding: because dbt-build is one opaque task consuming ~all ingestion, nearly every pre-dbt step is upstream; pruning=the slow post-dbt tail. Finer pruning needs dbt decomposition (deferred, H1+).

Proven by catching a REAL case: occurrences.db is nondeterministic across builds (see st-aji). Harness delivered; the nondeterminism it found is tracked separately.

title: Per-task hermetic runtime invocation (dual-interpreter)
description: Invoke each task in its own hermetic runtime. Concrete case: Python 3.14 for dlt loaders, 3.13 for dbt (dbt-core hard-crashes on 3.14). Secrets inject hermetically and must never reach logs.

Mechanism DELIVERED: per-task hermetic runtime invocation works; proven end-to-end on the uv/3.14 path (generate-sqlite). dbt uvx/3.13 runtime is declared + executor is runtime-agnostic (validated by construction), but not yet exercised by a real dbt run. Remaining under this umbrella: spot-run the dbt/3.13 path; multi-task plan execution (partial success = st-d44.4). Leaving open until the dbt path is actually exercised.
COMPLETE. dbt-build executed through Stelis in the uvx/3.13 runtime: 128 dbt nodes, exit 0 (3 warns, 0 errors). Dual-interpreter now proven end-to-end — generate-sqlite under uv/3.14 AND dbt-build under uvx/3.13 via the same runtime-agnostic run-task. beeatlas tracked tree stayed clean (duckdb + dbt target/ are gitignored derived state). Fixed main.rkt to only report occurrences.db for generate-sqlite.

title: Retire run.py's linear sequence for the occurrences.db build
description: Horizon 0 exit condition: naming occurrences.db builds the minimal upstream, skips already-current work, and retires run.py's hand-sequenced list for that build — reproducibly, verified by the determinism harness. Scope guard: transformations stay external (orchestrate dlt/dbt/exporters; do not reimplement in Racket).
notes:
Graph model decided via grilling — see docs/adr/0001-build-graph-model.md. Bipartite (task+artifact) nodes; gates=token-producing tasks; ingestion=opaque boundary artifacts (un-addressed, boundary-stamped); identity=authored name, version=separate/deferred; graph authored as Racket-native data in stelis. Slice 1 = plan only (1a plain Racket, 1b Datalog), full 33-node graph, precise edges for occurrences.db upstream.
EPIC COMPLETE (Horizon 0 build-system capabilities). Delivered: plan (bipartite graph, minimal-upstream, plain Racket + Datalog) -> dry-run commands -> execute per-task in hermetic runtimes (uv/3.14 + uvx/3.13) -> ordered plan execution with partial success + explicit output destinations -> input-addressed skip-if-current caching (derived/authoritative) -> streaming per-task logs -> determinism harness. Exit conditions: occurrences.db minimal-upstream rebuild PROVEN REPRODUCIBLE (byte-identical; beeatlas-zo7 fixed + verified); run.py's linear sequence retired for the derived tail. The only thing not run end-to-end is live ingestion (needs secrets — deferred, not a Stelis capability gap).
close_reason: Closed

title: Task code is not part of the input address: changing a recipe's script doesn't invalidate its cache
description: Found in production during st-vjd: notes_harvest.py changed (bakes body_md into notes.json), but the nightly cache-skipped notes-harvest ('cached — inputs unchanged') because the task's input address covers only its declared data inputs (collectors.json, notes-store.db) — not the code that produces the output. The stale notes.json shipped without body_md and the fix required manually destroying derived outputs to force a re-run. A task's recipe (script content, command line, runtime pin) IS an input to its output; content-address it like one. Options: hash the recipe's script file(s) into the task key; or at minimum the rendered command line + runtime identity. Relates to edge-verify's 'declared inputs sufficient?' question — this is the code-edge, not the data-edge.

title: Model Y gap: no code-only deploy path for beeatlas site changes
description:
Model Y step C deleted deploy.yml's deploy leg (push → GH Actions → S3/CloudFront), and nothing replaced it. The only publish paths on maderas are data/nightly.sh (full pipeline: stelis fetch-data, integration + JS data gates, render, merge-swap — also ingests fresh upstream data, so a code deploy is coupled to a data publish) and data/publish-notes.sh (notes-scoped, still runs a stelis build). Shipping a CSS/template/JS change today means either waiting for the 03:00 nightly's git pull or hand-running the whole nightly.

Proposed: a data/publish-code.sh sibling following the publish-notes.sh pattern — take the shared publish flock; git pull --ff-only; nvm use; npm ci only if the lockfile hash changed (reuse nightly's .npm-lock-hash cache); export EXPORT_DIR=$VAR_DIR/export so the render reuses the LAST-PUBLISHED data; run npm run test:data as a cheap gate (artifacts exist); npm run build; data/merge-swap.sh. No stelis invocation, no integration gate, no baseline restore/snapshot — the data tier is untouched, and identical export inputs produce identical hashed data artifacts.

Caveat to document in the script: a code change that alters the data CONTRACT (expects a new/changed export artifact) must ship via the full nightly instead — the stale export won't satisfy it.
notes: 2026-07-18: data/publish-code.sh written and verified locally end-to-end (scratch BASE_DIR, EXPORT_DIR=public/data, flock stubbed — flock is Linux-only, fine on maderas; same dependency as nightly.sh/publish-notes.sh). Sync→gate→build→merge-swap all exercised; scratch htdocs got the full page tree + hashed artifacts + manifest-last. Verification had to use VITEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 because of the PRE-EXISTING test/template contradiction filed separately (build-output.data.test.ts PAGE-07 vs ed4e269f's eager first hero). Remaining: commit (user drives), first real run on maderas.

Testable locally: racket src/main.rkt --build site --export-dir DIR.
close_reason: site task shipped: 11ty render is a Stelis graph node (24 inputs: 9 data artifacts + 15 scoped site-src leaves), output at EXPORT_DIR/site; beeatlas _data/scripts readers honor EXPORT_DIR via lib/build-data-dir.js (public/data fallback intact). Verified: 450/450 stelis tests; beeatlas vitest unchanged (17 pre-existing SW failures on clean tree too); --run site end-to-end green (full npm run build, 1668 pages, ~13s local); --commands site plans 29 tasks/7 pruned. Early-cutoff skip + maderas timing to be observed in Phase B (st-bgy).

title: [H2] CRUD trigger: write -> targeted notes rebuild -> publish
description: Gap 1 of the notes CRUD arc (st-066). RESHAPED by ADR 0007 Amendment (Model Y): the write path is 'commit -> shared flock -> npm run fetch-data notes-only (stelis --build --from notes-harvest --export-dir DIR notes.json, STELIS_REBUILD_KEYS=<canonical_name>) -> npm run build (full ~18s 11ty render) -> merge-swap _site into the served root -> respond live / saved-publish-pending'. SAME build+place the nightly runs (Model Y step C), scoped to notes. In api/main.py note-write routes (create/edit/delete/takedown/restore), after db_session.commit(): resolve canonical_name, take the flock, exec the shared publish script (env: BEEATLAS_DIR, DB_PATH persistent, NOTES_DB_PATH, EXPORT_DIR). Commit-first: build failure still succeeds the write with 'publish pending' + loud log; never roll back. Depends on Model Y C (shared build+place must exist first). PRODUCTION write path to the authoritative store — do with fresh context. Early-cutoff render-skip is gone (accepted).
notes: LANDED on main + LIVE on maderas 2026-07-17. Hand-run publish-notes.sh on maderas: 25.9s end-to-end (scoped stelis cached, render 22s, merge-swap 2s) — under Apache's default 60s proxy timeout. Real flock contention verified: held lock -> bounded wait -> exit 75, no build work. NOTE_PUBLISH_ENABLED=true in ~/.config/systemd/user/beeatlas-api.service, API restarted, healthy. REMAINING before close: (1) browser note-write round-trip by Peter (POST -> publish:live -> reload shows burned-in note); (2) sudo ProxyTimeout 300 on api.beeatlas.net vhost for the contended case (60s lock wait + 26s build > 60s default).

The FIRST production write exposed st-28p (keyed-store WAL input-addressing) — fixed in stelis 52c7276; the note then re-harvested targeted (STELIS_REBUILD_KEYS, 1 key) and is verified burned into https://beeatlas.net/species/Bombus/fervidus/ over HTTPS. Flock contention verified on maderas (bounded wait -> exit 75). Residual ops nit tracked in handoff: Apache ProxyTimeout 300 on api.beeatlas.net (sudo) for the contended-write case.

title: [H2.2b] Targeted execution: rebuild only changed keys of a fan-out
description:
Slice B of the H2 arc: a changed key rebuilds ONLY its output file, not the whole fan-out — the delta MECHANISM that H2.1 (reasoning) and H2.2a (notes observability) set up. First slice to change beeatlas mechanics (an exporter gains a partial mode); beeatlas is quiet so changes there are welcome.

CONTRACT (engine <-> exporter): the engine injects STELIS_REBUILD_KEYS (newline-separated, since keys like canonical_names contain spaces) when it elects a partial rebuild. An exporter that honors it emits ONLY those keys into the existing EXPORT_DIR (merge in place — untouched keys' files remain); one that ignores it does a full rebuild (correct, just not targeted). So it is an opt-in HINT, like the existing EXPORT_DIR/SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH injection.

MECHANISM: (1) engine computes changed output keys from the H2.1 prospective delta on the task's keyed inputs, mapped input-key->output-file via the keyed-by template (st-tul); (2) runs the task with STELIS_REBUILD_KEYS = added+changed; (3) DELETES files for removed keys (retraction); (4) the fan-out completeness gate re-validates the whole merged dir (already reads disk, so honest); (5) output-snapshot+keys records the merged per-key map. Precondition: a prior COMPLETE build exists to merge into, else full rebuild. Fallback: full rebuild is always correct.

INCREMENTS: B1 executor primitive — run-task honors #:rebuild-keys (injects the env var) + prune removed keys' files; hermetic synthetic-exporter test (identity key->path). B2 planner integration — decide partial vs full from the delta + keyed-by mapping. B3 wire a real beeatlas exporter (species-maps or feeds) to honor STELIS_REBUILD_KEYS; verify one changed key rebuilds one file. Later: notes path (needs notes.json restructure or the 11ty rebake as an emission node — the ADR 0013 subsumption payoff).
design: Env-var hint (STELIS_REBUILD_KEYS) over new recipe args: keeps the recipe stable and the exporter opt-in. Engine owns retraction (delete removed keys' files) since an exporter told 'rebuild key X' can't know X was removed. Completeness gate unchanged (reads the merged dir). B1 uses identity key->relpath; the keyed-by template mapping lands in B2.
acceptance_criteria: B1: run-task with #:rebuild-keys injects STELIS_REBUILD_KEYS; a synthetic exporter rebuilds only those keys; removed keys' files are pruned; merged dir is correct; hermetic (/bin/sh, no beeatlas). Existing tests green.
notes:
B3 DONE — targeted execution wired to a real exporter, verified end-to-end. beeatlas: notes_harvest.py writes per-species notes/<canonical_name>.json + honors STELIS_REBUILD_KEYS (partial: re-harvest only those); new assemble_notes.py rolls the dir into notes.json (11ty untouched); run.py gains notes-assemble; test_notes_harvest updated + partial test (beeatlas bd beeatlas-919, 6 tests pass). Stelis: beeatlas.rkt models notes ('dir) + notes-assemble, beeatlas-partial-tasks='(notes-harvest); main.rkt beeatlas-rebuild-keys-of folds the H1 prospective delta into (rebuild canonical_names . removed <name>.json) and passes #:rebuild-keys-of to run-plan; notes-digest.rkt now INNER-JOINs users to MATCH notes_harvest's keyset (found via end-to-end: an orphaned author_id diverged the two).

VERIFIED against the real store+exporters: full build writes 2 species; edit one note -> '⇒ partial: rebuilding 1 key(s)', notes-harvest writes only that species' file, the untouched species' file is byte-identical, notes.json reflects the edit. 436 Stelis + 6 beeatlas tests green.

NOT committed (either repo — user drives). Remaining for the full st-066 arc: the per-species PAGE rebake (11ty, ADR 0013 Layer 2) is the viewer-visible payoff — a future slice.

title: [H2.2a] Notes-store per-canonical_name observability (boundary CRUD-snapshot)
description:
First step of subsuming beeatlas's ADR 0013 notes worker into the general engine (user-confirmed arc, 2026-07-16: Stelis to cannibalize beeatlas mechanics incl. 11ty/dbt). Give the authoritative notes-store.db (a SQLite 'file leaf) a PER-canonical_name observation, so a CRUD on one note surfaces as a keyed delta and the H2.1 reasoning (--why/--history) names the changed species. Aligns the key with ADR 0013's incremental unit (canonical_name -> /species/<slug>/).

Notes schema (beeatlas notes_store/models.py): notes(id, canonical_name [indexed, NON-unique — stacked per species], author_id, body, body_html, status ['approved'|'pending'|'removed'], created_at, updated_at). notes.json = Record<canonical_name, Note[]>, APPROVED-only, species with zero approved notes absent as keys. So the per-key digest groups APPROVED notes by canonical_name and hashes the fields that flow into notes.json (id, body_html, author_id, created_at, updated_at) — a change/add/remove of a note moves exactly that species' key.

Pieces: (A1) a group-digest primitive over the SQLite store -> (canonical_name . digest) pairs, order-independent, via the shared DuckDB runner (sqlite scanner); testable against a fetched maderas copy + a synthetic fixture (duckdb-gated skip, like relation-digest-test). (A2) record that per-key map across builds for the INPUT leaf — the ingestion-boundary snapshot (st-066 open-Q1) — so prospective-delta has a 'from' basis; wire notes-store.db's live-key-map into delta-explain + --history.

NOT in scope: targeted execution (Slice B), the rebake/publish emission nodes, or touching beeatlas's worker.
design: A1 group-digest reuses duckdb.rkt; new module (notes-digest.rkt or store-digest.rkt). A2 is the new substrate: extend the observation recording so a declared keyed INPUT's per-key map is snapshotted into the build record (an input-key observation / boundary snapshot), giving H2.1's input-key-deltas a recorded basis. Key = canonical_name; digest over approved-note fields only, mirroring notes.json's keying exactly.
acceptance_criteria: group-digest yields (canonical_name . digest) over approved notes, order-independent, tested vs a synthetic SQLite fixture; a single-note edit/add/remove moves exactly the affected species' key; --history notes-store.db shows a per-species timeline; --why notes-harvest names the changed canonical_name(s) on a pending edit; hermetic (CI skips without duckdb); no beeatlas worker/exporter code touched.
notes:
A2 DONE + slice COMPLETE (2026-07-16). Approach (a): build-env resolve-store-keys slot mirrors resolve-relation-columns. Full path landed:
- cache.rkt: build-env gains resolve-store-keys; input-store-snapshot (the ingestion-boundary CRUD-snapshot — keyed STORE inputs of a task, sorted).
- trace.rkt: trace-record gains input-key-hashes (a producerless leaf's per-key map; a 'dir/db-relation input is observed by its producer, a store by nothing else).
- history.rkt: HISTORY-VERSION 2->3; history-key-observations now unions output + input key-hashes (trace-record-keyed).
- exec.rkt: records input-store-snapshot when a task runs (same point outputs are observed).
- delta-explain.rkt: live-key-map 'file branch -> resolve-store-keys.
- beeatlas.rkt: beeatlas-resolve-store-keys (notes-store.db -> notes-store-keys @ NOTES_DB_PATH); main.rkt wires the slot + --history 'keyed store' label.

VERIFIED end-to-end against the REAL fetched store + real beeatlas graph: record 5 species -> edit note 11 -> make-reason->string prints 'inputs changed: notes-store.db / notes-store.db -> 1 of 5 keys: ~bombus rufocinctus'. Exactly the slice goal. Full suite 418 green (13 new: 8 notes-digest, 5 store-path in delta-explain-test). No beeatlas worker/exporter touched.

Follow-ups: st-lg0 (kind->per-key-layer unification) now has THREE case sites (live-key-map, observe-output, input-store-snapshot logic) — pressure rising. Slice B (targeted execution) is next; notes is its first real consumer.
close_reason: Notes-store per-canonical_name observability complete: A1 group-digest (notes-digest.rkt) + A2 boundary-snapshot substrate (input-key-hashes on the trace, store resolver, history union). Verified end-to-end on the real store — edit one note, --why names the species. 418 tests green.

title: [H2.1] Per-key delta reasoning: fold key-observations into a named changed-key set
description:
First st-066 slice (delta REASONING before delta MECHANISM, mirroring H1's provenance-before-optimization sequence). Confirmed forks (user, 2026-07-16): (1) per-key staleness first, defer Z-sets/DBSP until coarse rebuilding demonstrably hurts (DESIGN 130-132); (2) first slice REPORTS the minimal changed key-set as the staleness reason, no beeatlas exporter changes.

Pure core: diff two consecutive key-observations of a keyed ('dir / db-relation) artifact into added/removed/changed key names. Retrospective source = history's key-observation timeline (folds the H1 observation history directly, per ROADMAP). Prospective source = live tree-hashes vs history tail. Reuses the exact same diff core (already present as changed-names in cache.rkt).

Surfaces: --history <artifact> annotates its hash timeline with per-key moves; --explain/--why refine 'input-changed prose from 'inputs changed: A' to 'A (2 of 214 keys: +olympia ~seattle)'. No transformation logic reimplemented (guardrail intact).
design: Build order: (1) pure key-delta struct + diff + timeline fold, tested in isolation; (2) retrospective wiring into --history; (3) prospective wiring into --explain/--why via a live tree-hashes read of the changed input. Keep the diff core shared with cache.rkt's changed-names rather than duplicating it.
acceptance_criteria: key-delta module + tests pass (raco test); --history <dir-artifact> shows per-key moves between consecutive re-productions; --why/--explain name the changed key subset for a keyed input-changed edge; no exporter/transformation code touched; existing tests still green.
notes:
DONE (2026-07-16). Prospective wiring landed via option (b): src/delta-explain.rkt — the sole impure seam. input-key-deltas reads a changed keyed input's LIVE map (tree-hashes for 'dir, resolve-relation-columns for 'db-relation) and diffs it against that input's history tail (delta.rkt prospective-delta). make-reason->string decorates the reason prose; explain.rkt print-explanations and provenance-datalog.rkt print-why-tree now take an optional #:reason->string (default = pure decision->string, so behaviour is unchanged unless opted in). main.rkt --explain and --why pass the decorated renderer.

Tests: delta-test (pure core, incl. prospective-delta) + delta-explain-test (real history round-trip + real tree-hashes IO). Full suite 406 green. --why against the real beeatlas graph runs clean; decorator is correctly a no-op with no key-observation history (existing output identical). The prospective subset line will print once a real CRUD-then-rebuild leaves a keyed input changed vs history — that in-vivo observation belongs to the mechanism slice.

Acceptance met: module+tests green; --history shows per-key moves (inherited from st-6dv, now routed through the shared core); --why/--explain name the changed key subset for a keyed 'input-changed edge; no exporter/transformation code touched; existing tests green. NOT committed (user drives git).
close_reason: Per-key delta reasoning slice complete: pure core (delta.rkt) + impure --why/--explain adapter (delta-explain.rkt), retrospective --history consolidated onto the shared core, 406 tests green. Delta MECHANISM (targeted execution) remains under st-066.

title: Integrity gate: block the build on anomalous record-count deltas vs history
description: OPERATOR-facing pipeline-integrity check (H1). A build whose observed record count deviates sharply from the previous build's (e.g. a source broke, a join exploded/collapsed) should BLOCK — this is what gate semantics (st-d44.4) are actually for, as opposed to editorial flags (st-8v3) which don't block. The check is HISTORY-RELATIVE: it compares this build's observation to the previous build's, making it the second real consumer of the st-sds observation substrate (after st-066). Fits ADR 0005: consults the sequence, but as an operator anomaly alarm, never a freshness verdict (freshness stays content-hash+graph, clockless). Guardrail-clean: reasons ABOUT the build's data volumes, reimplements no transform. Prereq: observe row count(*) per relation in history (the digest currently computes count(*) but hashes it away). Open forks: (a) build-driver policy vs first-class in-process gate node; (b) where per-relation thresholds live.
close_reason: Integrity gate shipped, done the node way. New in-process rule-check modality in exec.rkt (a rule evaluated in Racket as a graph node, gating downstream via the existing blocker flow) — the reusable 'rules as nodes' foundation for the data-quality Datalog line. First rule = the integrity gate in data-quality.rkt: current record count (live DuckDB) vs previous build's (history) blocks publish on a swing beyond threshold (default 50%). row count(*) now observed per relation (relation-row-count + '.*' observation part). Wired into beeatlas: inat-obs-integrity gate guards inat_obs_data before dbt-build. Validated end-to-end vs real beeatlas.duckdb (28850 rows: +3% pass, +188%/-52% block). 363 tests pass. NOTE: the integrity rule's logic is arithmetic (Datalog has no arithmetic) — the rule-NODE mechanism is the Datalog-enabling investment; the first genuinely relational/Datalog rule-sets are the editorial checks (st-8v3, deferred). Unreadable current count passes-with-warning (degrade, don't halt) — a policy choice worth revisiting.

title: [H2] Fast targeted rebuild driven by CRUD on authoritative stores
description:
Near-term GOAL (user, 2026-07-14): a CRUD operation on an authoritative store (e.g. the notes store notes-harvest reads) should trigger a FAST, TARGETED rebuild of just the affected derived artifacts, not a full graph rebuild. May require beeatlas to change some of its mechanics (how it stores/ingests).

HORIZON: this is Horizon 2 (ROADMAP: 'Streaming / CRUD ingestion — small frequent content-addressed snapshots at the ingestion boundary; near-real-time incorporation' + 'Delta-based propagation'). Flagged as a horizon jump from H1 per CLAUDE.md; scope before building.

SUBSTRATE ALREADY IN PLACE (H1, deliberately shaped for this): the keyed-by fan-out declaration (st-tul) is chosen so promoting each key to an independently-stale artifact = delta propagation; the 'dir tree digest doubles as a realized manifest; notes.json being derived (st-msn) is what makes on-demand rebuild of it legal at all. So H1 work is pointed here; what remains for H2 is CRUD snapshots at the boundary + per-key/delta propagation + the beeatlas mechanics changes.
design: OPEN QUESTIONS to resolve before building: (1) what CRUD-snapshot shape at the ingestion boundary (salishsea's model?); (2) delta representation (Z-sets/DBSP-shaped per ROADMAP, or simpler per-key staleness first?); (3) which beeatlas mechanics must change (notes store emitting change events? content-addressed snapshots?); (4) does per-key artifact promotion reuse the fan-out keyed-by declaration directly, or need a richer key identity.

title: Declared fan-out key: verify data-dependent output SET completeness
description:
Shape 1b of st-4cm output-shape model. Upgrade a 'dir output from opaque to a verified data-dependent set.

Let a producer declare that its directory is keyed by a column of a NAMED INPUT relation, e.g. species-maps emits one file per species.json[].slug. Carry as an artifact field (e.g. #:keyed-by (input-artifact . key-path)).

edge-verify (st-qp7) then checks a real invariant: {output files in the dir} == {keys of the input relation}, upgrading from the opaque 'dir non-empty' check. Stays one artifact / one producer / one rebuild — NO dynamic graph nodes (that is H2 delta propagation).

Chosen so H2 consumes the same declaration: promoting each key to an independently-stale artifact = delta propagation, later.
design: Depends on the 'dir kind + tree digest bead landing first (the tree digest provides the {output files} set to compare against input keys). H2 delta propagation reuses the same keyed-by declaration.
close_reason: Declared fan-out key: 'dir artifacts gain #:keyed-by (list of fan-out branches: input, key-column, filename-template). New fan-out-key.rkt verifies files ⊆ input keys (soundness GATED) and reports keys-without-files (completeness). Wired place-maps (keyed by places.json[].slug); validated on real build: 132/181 sound, 49 filtered reported. Multi-branch supported+unit-tested for species-maps' shape. species-maps/feeds stay opaque 'dir until wired (species-maps needs species.parquet declared — follow-on). Slice under open epic st-4cm.

title: Directory artifact kind ('dir) + order-independent tree digest
description:
Shape 1a of st-4cm output-shape model. Add a 'dir artifact kind alongside 'file so directory/data-dependent outputs (species-maps, place-maps, feeds) become buildable+cacheable.

- model.rkt: 'dir as an artifact kind.
- beeatlas.rkt: beeatlas-path gains a 'dir case resolving to a directory under EXPORT_DIR (these currently return #f, so st-6qc refuses them). Declare species-maps/place-maps/feeds outputs as 'dir artifacts.
- cache.rkt + relation-digest.rkt: content-address a 'dir by an order-independent digest over sorted (relative-path -> content-hash) pairs — same order-independent philosophy relation-digest already uses for db-relations. Receipts store the tree digest where 'file stores one hash.
- Determinism check: build twice, compare tree digests.

This makes the dir buildable and content-addressed but treats it as opaque; completeness verification is the follow-on (fan-out key) bead.
design: Tree digest is a sorted map (relative-path -> content-hash); it doubles as the realized output manifest that the fan-out-key completeness check (follow-on) and eventual H2 delta propagation build on.
close_reason: 'dir artifact kind + order-independent tree digest (tree-digest.rkt); species-maps/place-maps/feeds now buildable, content-addressed, cacheable. Determinism verified on a real 591-file species-maps build. Slice under open epic st-4cm.

title: Edge-verification gate: check a task's declared inputs are sufficient and outputs complete
description: THROUGH-LINE from st-4cm slices 1-3: every post-dbt edge is a hand-transcribed (lossy) copy of what the Python task actually reads/writes, and it stays subtly wrong until a target exercises it. Each slice rediscovered the same class of bug (sandbox-vs-@export inputs, missing inputs, an undeclared output) the same way: run the task hermetically, watch a FileNotFoundError, or notice an extra written file. Mechanize that loop into a check. Task already runs in a controlled EXPORT_DIR, so: (a) INPUTS SUFFICIENT — seed the hermetic run with ONLY the task's declared inputs and assert it succeeds (a missing declared input = under-approximated edge); (b) OUTPUTS COMPLETE — assert it wrote exactly its declared outputs, no more/less (catches undeclared outputs like place_details.json in slice 3). Converts per-target archaeology into a red/green gate; makes under-declared inputs AND outputs impossible to miss. Retro-validate the shipped targets (occurrences.db, species.json, collectors.json, places.json). Highest-leverage of the three cleanups. Horizon check: batch, provenance-adjacent, no change-over-time = Horizon 1 in character; the user is the one paying the transcription tax, so not premature-for-lack-of-a-user, but confirm scope before building (edges toward a general edge-verification feature).
close_reason: Built edge-verify.rkt harness: runs a task in a fresh EXPORT_DIR seeded with ONLY its declared @export inputs, asserts it still succeeds (inputs sufficient) and wrote exactly its declared outputs (outputs complete). Fixed-path inputs (sandbox/DB/seeds) are ambient. Pure classify-outputs + export-dir-artifact? unit-tested (edge-verify-test.rkt); gated integration test retro-validates the 4 shipped terminals (edge-verify-integration-test.rkt, skips w/o a reference build). Retro-validation FOUND a real slice-1 gap: species-export writes 6 files, edge declared 2 — added seasonality/photos/species_hosts/higher_taxa.json outputs+artifacts+paths; now 4/4 clean. No CLI (per decision). 224 tests pass.

title: Incremental rebuild via early cutoff: stop propagation on identical output
description: Second H1 slice (ROADMAP.md). Today a task whose input CHANGED always reruns everything downstream, even if its rebuilt output is byte-identical to before. Outputs are already content-hashed (determinism harness); extend the cache entry with output hashes and, after a task runs, compare — if outputs are unchanged, downstream tasks see unchanged inputs and cache-skip naturally. Watch the derived-vs-authoritative rule: cutoff applies only to derived outputs. Sequenced after the provenance epic so --explain can show exactly where cutoff triggered ('reran, output identical, downstream skipped').
acceptance_criteria: A change that rebuilds an intermediate to identical content does not rerun downstream tasks; --explain reports the cutoff point; determinism harness still passes.
close_reason: Cutoff is emergent from input-addressing (run-plan re-decides each task after upstreams ran); implemented the receipt: cache entry v3 stores per-output content hashes (derived+resolvable only, per the derived-vs-authoritative rule), post-run comparison yields an output-delta ('identical = the cutoff point) recorded on trace v3, printed during --build and rendered by --explain --last. Snapshot-less tasks (boundary, dbt) now store outputs-only entries so their reruns have a comparison basis. End-to-end acceptance test in src/exec-test.rkt; ADR 0003.

title: Slice 4 — record the build trace: --explain answers about the last real build
description: Slices 1–3 explain a hypothetical (dry-run) build. This slice persists what a real --build actually decided and did — per-task decision, outcome (ok/cached/failed/skipped), and fingerprints — as a small versioned trace file under .stelis/ (derived, disposable state, same rules as the cache sidecars). '--explain --last' then answers 'why DID that rebuild?' from the trace. This is deliberately the thin end of H1 trace/graph persistence: choose the record shape so the fuller persistence work can adopt it rather than replace it.
acceptance_criteria: After a --build, --explain --last reports each task's actual decision and outcome from the persisted trace without re-fingerprinting; trace format is versioned and a stale/unparseable trace degrades gracefully.
close_reason: run-plan records per-task decision+outcome; trace.rkt persists them versioned under .stelis/; --explain --last replays without re-fingerprinting; graceful degradation tested; 151 tests pass

title: Slice 3 — provenance as Datalog: stale/why-stale as rules over decision facts
description: Third Datalog application. Emit slice 1's decision records as Datalog facts (input-changed(T,A), boundary(T), cached(T), depends(T,U), ...) and define staleness by rules — e.g. stale(T) :- input-changed(T,_); stale(T) :- depends(T,U), stale(U) — so 'why is X stale?' becomes a query whose derivation IS the provenance chain, transitively (slice 2 only explains one hop). Follow the slice-1a/1b pattern from st-d44: cross-check the Datalog answer against the plain-Racket explanation on the beeatlas graph and on synthetic graphs. Keep it metadata-only per the H0 commitment: Datalog reasons ABOUT the build, transformations stay external.
acceptance_criteria: A Datalog query answers stale?/why-stale? for any task with a full transitive chain; answers cross-checked against the plain-Racket path in tests.
close_reason: staleness as Datalog rules over decision facts; blames/2 gives the transitive chain; --why CLI renders it as a tree; cross-checked against walk-explanations on synthetic + beeatlas graphs; 141 tests pass

title: Streaming per-task observability
description: Streaming, per-task logs by default. Secrets must never reach logs.
notes: DONE. run-task #:label captures the child's stdout/stderr and re-emits each line prefixed with the task name (streaming, per-task, real-time; separate pump threads so neither stream deadlocks). run-plan labels every task; single --run stays unlabeled (inherits stdio). Demonstrated: '  generate-sqlite │ ...'. 56 tests.
close_reason: Closed

title: Gates as first-class nodes; partial success; explicit output destinations
description: Gates/assertions as first-class, cacheable, skippable graph nodes. Partial success: a failed target must not fail unrelated already-satisfied targets. Explicit output destinations — no hidden copies.
notes: DONE. run-plan (exec.rkt) executes an ordered plan with partial success on a pure blockers-of core (failed producer blocks dependents; ok doesn't; unrelated failures don't). main.rkt --build <target> [--from <task>]. Explicit output destination via EXPORT_DIR (scratch). Demonstrated: --build --from dbt-build occurrences.db ran dbt-build[uvx/3.13] -> generate-sqlite[uv/3.14] in order, 2 ok, output to scratch, beeatlas tree clean. Partial-success semantics unit-tested (54 checks). Gates execute as ordinary tasks (a nonzero exit -> 'failed -> blocks dependents); no gate sits on the runnable derived tail so gate-failure isn't live-demoed, but the mechanism is uniform + tested. --from is a temporary suffix-bound until skip-if-current (st-d44.3) lands.
close_reason: Closed

title: Input-addressed task caching + derived-vs-authoritative escape hatch
description: Cache task results by content hash of inputs. Derived outputs are safe to destroy and rebuild; authoritative state is forward-only (migrations only, never rebuilt from scratch). The caching model must express both node types.
notes: DONE (first increment). Input-addressed skip-if-current caching. src/cache.rkt: input-fingerprint = sha1(recipe + per-input-file content hashes); returns #f for tasks whose inputs aren't fully content-addressable (duckdb relations/tokens) — those always run. Disposable versioned sidecars in gitignored .stelis/cache/ (format churn -> cache miss, never breakage; safe to rm). run-plan (exec.rkt) skips a task as 'cached when fp matches AND outputs still exist; blockers-of treats 'cached as satisfied. Derived-vs-authoritative: added provenance field to artifacts (model.rkt), notes.json marked authoritative (addresses code-review #5); the escape-hatch runtime guard activates when an authoritative output is on a runnable path (none on occurrences.db). Demonstrated on generate-sqlite: cold->runs, warm->cached, touch(mtime)->still cached (content-addressed), output-deleted->re-runs. dbt-build stays uncached (duckdb inputs, per ADR-0001 Q4 / H2 snapshotting). 56 tests. Advances epic exit-condition 'skips current work'.
close_reason: Closed

title: 2 dbt diff tests skip every night: species/seasonality JSON byte-compares look in the retired run.py sandbox path
description: test_dbt_diff.py test_species_json_matches + test_seasonality_json_matches skipif SANDBOX/species.json missing ('run species JSON post-step first'). Under run.py the post-step wrote these into data/dbt/target/sandbox; under Stelis the exporters write to EXPORT_DIR, so the files never exist in SANDBOX and both tests skip silently every nightly (visible as the standing '58 passed, 2 skipped' gate line). Not a simple repoint: they assert BYTE EQUALITY vs last-published (public/data), which would fail the gate on any legitimate data change — decide whether to (a) repoint at EXPORT_DIR and convert to the drift-tolerance idiom the other diff tests use, or (b) delete as vestigial run.py-era determinism checks. Discovered during the 2026-07-18 soak-watch review of the first cron nightly.
close_reason: (a) repoint+convert. test_dbt_diff.py now resolves exporter artifacts via $EXPORT_DIR (new EXPORT constant) instead of the retired run.py SANDBOX path. The 2 byte-compares became 5 drift-idiom tests: species.json entry-count band, canonical_name anti-join, strict entry key-set; seasonality.json species count+key drift, and a structural well-formedness check (bucket prefixes, int[12] histograms). Guard skips loudly if EXPORT_DIR is unset/==public/data rather than comparing a file to itself. Verified against a real fresh export: 5/5 pass; nightly gate goes 58p+2s -> 63p. Dropped a proposed seasonality._total == species.month_histogram coherence test: FALSE invariant (month_histogram sums ecdysis+checklist months per int_species_universe.sql; seasonality counts occurrence rows only).

title: beeatlas js-tests red on main: 17 pre-existing prime/sw env failures (caches.open undefined in CI jsdom)
description: Known since Model Y step B verification: vitest suite = 958 pass + 17 env failures (prime-orchestrator/sw/species tests) on clean HEAD and locally. Now that deploy.yml is deleted and js-tests.yml is the surviving CI leg (Model Y C), these failures turn the workflow permanently red on main, masking real regressions. Fix the test environment (Cache API / service-worker mocks for jsdom — caches.open is not a function, DOMException AbortError storms) or quarantine the 17 with an env guard so the leg is green and meaningful.

title: [H2] Explore: editorial data-quality flags as the first sentential-DB value prop

Explore what a flags feature needs from Stelis one or two steps out from what exists, and whether a Datalog-queried sentential substrate earns its way in through it. title: Cross-repo contract test: notes-digest keyset == notes_harvest output
description: Code-review finding (st-pd1 Standards 7): notes-digest.rkt hardcodes beeatlas's users JOIN + note field list, mirroring notes_harvest's query. A field added in notes_harvest silently stales the digest with no test tying them. Add a contract test (ideally cross-repo, or a shared fixture) asserting notes-store-keys' species set matches notes_harvest's emitted files for the same store.

title: Partial-rebuild refactors: dedup decision, home the key→relpath map
description: Code-review findings (st-pd1 Standards 4,5,6): (1) main.rkt beeatlas-rebuild-keys-of recomputes decision+snapshot that run-plan also computes — pass the decision into #:rebuild-keys-of instead of recomputing. (2) the key→'<name>.json' mapping lives in 3 places (main.rkt, notes_harvest, assemble_notes); give it a named home (beeatlas.rkt owns beeatlas shape). (3) run-plan's run branch is accreting (Long Function) — consider extracting the partial-rebuild orchestration.

title: Notes fan-out completeness gate (keyed-by against the store keyset)
description: Code-review finding (st-pd1 Spec a1): the 'notes 'dir has no keyed-by, so verify-fan-out-keys never validates the merged notes/ set. A partial rebuild's soundness is unchecked. notes-store-keys is available for an identity check (files ⊆ store canonical_names). Add a keyed-by (or an equivalent identity gate) so the merged dir is validated, and tighten the 'prior COMPLETE build' precondition beyond directory-exists.

title: Unify the artifact-kind to per-key-layer seam (live vs recorded)
description:
Code-review finding (st-2hh Standards axis, 2026-07-16): delta-explain.rkt live-key-map and cache.rkt observe-output both dispatch on artifact-kind over the same dir / db-relation / else arms, with parallel tree-hashes / resolve-relation-columns logic. Two readers of one concept -- 'given an artifact, what is its per-key (part to value) layer?' -- one reading it LIVE off disk (delta-explain), one reading the RECORDED digest after a run (cache). Repeated Switches plus latent Shotgun Surgery: adding a new keyed kind means editing both sites, and they can silently diverge.

Totality/exhaustiveness checking will NOT catch a missed kind here (neither plain Racket nor Typed Racket checks case/match exhaustiveness -- TR narrows via occurrence typing but does not error on an omitted arm; and else-to-#f is a legitimate 'no per-key layer' answer, so a loud else is wrong). So the fix is STRUCTURAL, not type-driven: a single kind-to-per-key-layer seam both callers share (data-driven dispatch table on artifact-kind, or gen: generic dispatch per kind).

Not urgent (only two arms today). Do when a third keyed kind lands or when next touching either site. Keep the two DISTINCT value sources (live tree-hashes/columns vs recorded digest) behind one kind-dispatch, not merged into one function.
design: Candidate: a shared (artifact-kind -> extractor) table or gen: interface that both live-key-map (delta-explain.rkt) and observe-output (cache.rkt) consult, so a new kind is one edit. Preserve the live-vs-recorded distinction.
acceptance_criteria: One place enumerates keyed kinds and their per-key extractor; delta-explain.rkt live-key-map and cache.rkt observe-output both dispatch through it; adding a keyed kind touches exactly one site; existing tests green.
close_reason: Unified the kind->per-key-layer dispatch into cache.rkt artifact-key-parts; live-key-map, observe-output (parts), and input-store-snapshot all route through it. A new keyed kind is now one case. Behaviour-preserving, 418 green.

title: Per-key observations: fan-out 'dir outputs record per-file hash timelines
description: Second st-sds slice. Retain the (relative-path -> content-hash) pairs tree-digest already computes for 'dir outputs, record them in history alongside the rolled-up artifact hash, and surface a per-key timeline (--history <dir-artifact> shows which keys changed at each build). Keyed by relative path (decode to domain key deferred to H2 propagation, which reads the input relation anyway). Stays content+graph, linear time. Layers: tree-hashes in tree-digest.rkt; output-snapshot+keys in cache.rkt; output-key-hashes field on trace-record; run-plan capture; history-key-observations; --history rendering; observed-key/4 Datalog projection.
close_reason: Per-key observations shipped: 'dir outputs record per-file (path->hash) timelines; --history <dir> attributes changes to specific keys; observed-key/4 Datalog projection. Keyed by relative path (domain-key decode deferred to H2). 327 tests pass.

title: History: append-only observation store + graph snapshot (st-sds first slice)
description: First slice of st-sds. Build history.rkt (append-only, content-addressed build-record store under .stelis/, adopting trace.rkt's record shapes enriched with output-hashes), a per-graph snapshot + graph-digest in model.rkt, wire --build to append, retire last-build.rktd into history, extend provenance-datalog.rkt to project history facts, and add --history CLI. Freshness stays content-hash + graph; sequence is for browsing only. Defers propagation (st-066), per-key observations, SQLite, refs/branches (H3).
notes: Shipped. New src/history.rkt: append-only .stelis/history.rktd (one versioned build per line; a corrupt/stale line is skipped, never fatal) + once-per-topology graphs/<hash>.rktd snapshot. build-record {target, graph-hash, epoch, records}; records are trace.rkt's trace-record ENRICHED with output-hashes (the artifact->hash observation). API: history-append!/-load/-last/-observations/-graph. model.rkt gained graph->datum + graph-digest (topology snapshot, recipes/keyed-by omitted). trace.rkt retired last-build.rktd storage, keeping only the record struct + trace-record->datum/datum->trace-record (shared with history). exec.rkt run-plan records output-hashes. provenance-datalog.rkt history->theory projects observed/3, ran/2, derived-from/3 (+ datalog-observations, datalog-derived-from). main.rkt: --build appends to history, --explain --last reads history tail, new --history mode. Line held: freshness never consults the sequence. Tests: history-test.rkt, updated trace-test.rkt, projection block in provenance-test.rkt (316 pass). Deferred per st-sds: per-key observations, propagation (st-066), SQLite, refs/branches (H3).
close_reason: First slice of st-sds implemented and tested end-to-end

title: feeds fan-out verification via beeatlas-emitted key→file manifest
description:
Split from st-5jt. feeds writes collector-{slugify(recorded_by)}.xml and genus-{slugify(genus)}.xml, with collision disambiguation (seen_slugs, feeds.py:281). So {file keys} = slugify(distinct column) WITH suffixing — not a column value. Reproducing slugify+dedup in Racket would reimplement beeatlas logic (violates DESIGN 'transformations stay external') and risk divergence.

DECISION (2026-07-14): beeatlas EMITS the ground-truth manifest; the fan-out-key harness consumes it (no Racket re-derivation). feeds already writes feeds/index.json enumerating every variant feed with metadata (write_index_json) — likely 90% of the needed manifest; extend it to map each produced file → its source (variant_type, filter_value, slug). Harness then checks {produced files} == {manifest entries} and each entry's filter_value is a real distinct column value. Requires a beeatlas feeds.py change. Depends on the singleton-file + manifest-source machinery. determinations.xml/index.json are the un-keyed singletons to allow.
close_reason: feeds verified via its emitted manifest (feeds/index.json) — NO beeatlas change needed: index.json already maps filename→verbatim filter_value + filter_type. New manifest-key declaration + verify-manifest: checks {files}=={manifest entries}∪{singletons} AND each entry's filter_value is a real DISTINCT value of its ecdysis_data source column (via duckdb). 232/232 sound, 0 incomplete (feeds writes one per distinct collector/genus, unfiltered). Consolidated SQL-safety patterns into duckdb.rkt. All 3 dir terminals now set-verified (place-maps/species-maps/feeds). 292 tests.

title: Reconcile notes.json provenance with st-4cm Shape 3 (derived, not authoritative)
description:
st-4cm's Shape 3 (decided 2026-07-12) says notes-harvest READS the authoritative notes STORE read-only and emits a DERIVED notes.json (safe to destroy, reproducible); the authoritative thing is the INPUT store, which should be modeled as a boundary input (like raw-file-paths), optionally file-hashed so cutoff notices store changes.

The code predates that decision and still marks notes.json #:provenance 'authoritative (beeatlas.rkt ~L286), with a comment claiming it 'addresses the derived-vs-authoritative commitment.' That contradicts the settled Shape 3 resolution. Currently harmless (notes.json is pruned from occurrences.db and never built/verified), but it's a DESIGN-vs-code inconsistency.

Fix: relabel notes.json 'derived; add a boundary-input artifact for the external notes store (data/artifacts.toml or the notes SQLite) as notes-harvest's real authoritative input; keep notes.json as an ordinary derived single-file output. Parity caveat (from Shape 3): notes.json is input-drift-prone like collectors.json — direct-invocation parity on the same store snapshot is the right determinism check.
design: DECISION (2026-07-14): notes.json is DERIVED — override beeatlas's data/artifacts.toml 'authoritative' label. It IS reproducible from the notes store, and the near-term goal of FAST TARGETED REBUILD in response to CRUD on the store requires it be rebuildable (a forward-only authoritative output cannot be rebuilt on demand). The authoritative thing is the INPUT: the notes store (SQLite via notes_store.db.make_engine, NOTES_DB_PATH) — currently an UNDECLARED input of notes-harvest (which also reads collectors.json, already declared). H1 scope here: relabel notes.json 'derived; declare the notes store as a boundary INPUT node (content-hash the .db for cutoff). The provenance axis staying output-only is fine — the store is an external input, modeled like geographies/raw files. The CRUD→targeted-rebuild capability itself is H2 (see the new epic) and may need beeatlas mechanics changes; this bead only does the unblocking H1 relabel + edge fix.
close_reason: notes.json relabeled 'derived (was mislabeled 'authoritative); the authoritative label moved to the INPUT notes store, modeled as a producerless 'file leaf at NOTES_DB_PATH (forward-only by construction, content-hashed when present). Fixed notes-harvest's under-declared edge (the store was missing). notes.json is now cutoff-eligible — the hook the CRUD→targeted-rebuild goal (st-066) needs. 287 tests. No beeatlas change; store not mounted locally so runtime gating N/A.

title: Fan-out keys for species-maps (composite + parquet-column sources)
description:
st-tul's fan-out-key model is single-column: one file per value of one column of a NAMED INPUT relation. species-maps and feeds need richer declarations to be SET-verified (they currently stay opaque 'dir, tree-digest only):

- COMPOSITE keys: species-maps' subgenus/<Genus>/<Subgenus>.svg is keyed by (genus, subgenus) — needs a multi-column key / multi-{} template.
- DB-RELATION key sources: species-maps' per-species branch keys off species.parquet@export; feeds' fan-outs key off distinct ecdysis_data.occurrences.recorded_by (collector) and .genus. Key extraction must read a parquet/db-relation column, not only a JSON array.
- SINGLETON files: feeds/determinations.xml and feeds/index.json are always-present, un-keyed — need an 'always' branch so they aren't flagged orphans.

Then wire species-maps (5 branches) and feeds (2 fan-outs + 2 singletons) and add them to the fan-out-key integration test. H2 delta propagation consumes the same enriched declaration.
design:
DECISION (2026-07-14): SPLIT — this bead now covers species-maps ONLY. Its branches are all VERBATIM column values (no transform), so verifiable in Racket:
- composite key: subgenus/<Genus>/<Subgenus>.svg keyed by (genus, subgenus) → multi-{} template + column list; file->key/classify become tuple-valued.
- db-relation/parquet key source: per-species <slug>.svg keyed off species.parquet@export slug column (verbatim, Pitfall #3) via DuckDB column extraction (reuse relation-digest.rkt CLI path); group ranks (genus/tribe/subfamily) keyed off species.json columns.
- singleton handling not needed here (species-maps has no un-keyed files).
feeds moved to its own bead: its keys are slugify(column) with collision dedup, not verifiable this way.
notes:
DONE (2026-07-14). CORRECTION to the design note: all FIVE branches key off species.parquet@export (the group-membership query reads genus/subgenus/tribe/subfamily/slug straight from that parquet) — species-maps never opens species.json, so the earlier 'group ranks off species.json' note was wrong. Wired: slug '{}.svg', genus 'genus/{}.svg', subgenus (composite genus,subgenus) 'subgenus/{}/{}.svg', tribe 'tribe/{}.svg', subfamily 'subfamily/{}.svg'. Verified sound against the reference: 785/785 files keyed, 82 incomplete (filtered) — composite matched with plain DISTINCT, no trim needed (data clean).

Extensions delivered: fan-out branch key is now a COLUMN LIST (tuple key); file->key returns a tuple via a per-template regex; json-column-keys + new parquet-column-keys (DuckDB) both composite; column-keys dispatches by .json/.parquet extension. Extracted the shared DuckDB CLI runner into duckdb.rkt (relation-digest now reuses it). 288 tests; fan-out integration now 2/2 (place-maps + species-maps).
close_reason: species-maps fan-out wired + verified (785/785 sound). Composite keys, parquet key source, tuple core delivered; duckdb.rkt extracted and shared. Unblocks st-q6i (feeds manifest).

title: Determinism-preserving policy for timestamps/build-time in outputs
description:
RECURRING HAZARD (first hit: beeatlas-8td, topology _meta.built_at). Build outputs legitimately want provenance metadata — 'built_at', a build id, etc. — but any wall-clock value breaks the day-one determinism commitment (DESIGN: 'Content-addressed, not timestamped'): the same snapshot built twice yields different bytes, so the cache never gets early cutoff and --verify fails. This will keep happening as more targets are authored; we need a defined approach, not per-case stripping.

The decision to make: when a task's output embeds a time (or any 'build identity'), WHAT value does it embed so that it's a deterministic function of the snapshot, not of wall-clock?

Options:
1. Deterministic build clock (SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH-style). Stelis injects ONE build-wide epoch into every task's hermetic env, derived from the snapshot's provenance (e.g. the latest source commit's committer date). Any script that wants built_at reads it from the env instead of time.time(). Every rebuild of the same snapshot stamps the same value -> deterministic AND still meaningful provenance. This is the reproducible-builds industry convention and composes with the existing hermetic-env injection. LEANING toward this.
2. Content/commit-derived stamps only: outputs stamp git_sha (already done) and/or commit date, never build time. Simpler but relies on every script author remembering.
3. Canonicalize-before-hash: content-address outputs with known-volatile fields excluded. Fragile — needs a per-format volatile-field registry; also the on-disk bytes still differ (only the hash is stable), so it doesn't fix --verify's byte compare.

Cross-cutting with provenance (provenance-datalog.rkt) and the content-addressing commitment. beeatlas-8td is the concrete instance; this bead is the general stelis policy that should inform how we fix it (prefer option 1: give beeatlas a stelis-injected SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH rather than a beeatlas-local hack). Record the outcome as an ADR under docs/adr/ once decided.
design: Leaning: option 1 — a single stelis-injected, snapshot-derived build epoch (SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH convention) consumed by any task that needs a build time. Preserves provenance without sacrificing determinism; composes with the existing per-task hermetic env injection (exec.rkt). Decide + ADR before fixing beeatlas-8td's built_at so the fix uses the policy, not a local workaround.
notes: SHIPPED (stelis side): ADR 0004 (docs/adr/0004-build-clock.md) decides a deterministic build clock. Implementation: beeatlas-source-date-epoch (= beeatlas HEAD committer date, honors preset SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH) in beeatlas.rkt; main.rkt task-env injects it into every executed task's hermetic env (--run/--build) and --verify passes it as #:extra-env to both builds; determinism.rkt gained #:extra-env. Clock is AMBIENT (not in recipe-hash/input-snapshot) to avoid global invalidation on every commit — no cache/trace format change. Verified: epoch reaches subprocess end-to-end, numeric+stable (plan-test #11). The concrete instance fix (topology reading the var) is tracked in beeatlas-8td SITE 1 with the exact contract; end-to-end --verify demonstration follows when that lands.
close_reason: Stelis-side timestamp policy shipped: ADR 0004 + SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH injection into every task + determinism harness support + test. Concrete beeatlas built_at fix carried by beeatlas-8td SITE 1 (contract recorded there).

title: Fail loudly when a declared target output resolves to no path (beeatlas-path #f)
description: From st-4cm slice-3 review. beeatlas-path/case maintains a terminal-export enum [(occurrences.db species.json collectors.json places.json ...)] that is appended per slice. Forgetting to add a new target does NOT error — it falls to [else #f], silently making the artifact uncacheable and unverifiable; you only notice via the enum. A DECLARED TARGET OUTPUT whose path resolves to #f should be a hard error at build/plan time, not a silent skip. Small, pure-safety guardrail. Partly subsumed by the placement-as-property refactor but worth doing independently since it is trivial and removes a real footgun.
close_reason: Guard added: check-file-outputs-resolvable (cache.rkt) rejects any plan whose 'file output resolves to #f, wired into --build and --verify (main.rkt). feeds now errors loudly; shipped targets pass. plan-test #11 covers both cases.

title: Model artifact placement as a property, not a scattered convention
description: From st-4cm slice-3 review. The sandbox<->export-dir duality is currently expressed in FIVE parallel places in beeatlas.rkt: mart-files, export-name, the @export regex in beeatlas-path, the hand-maintained terminal-export enum in beeatlas-path/case, and the cp loop in place-marts-script. Key fact: the @export copy is byte-identical to its sandbox original BY CONSTRUCTION (place-marts is a plain cp) — so this is not a content distinction, it is pure PLACEMENT. Fold it: let a file artifact declare where it lives (sandbox / export-dir / raw) and, for a placed copy, which sandbox artifact it copies. Then place-marts, the @export artifacts, and their on-disk paths all DERIVE from that one declaration instead of being maintained in parallel. Fewer edits per new target; the sandbox-vs-export distinction becomes explicit instead of hiding in a string suffix. RECOMMENDATION: hold until the edge-verification gate exists — that gate will reveal whether the placement model captures the right things before this refactor commits to a shape.
close_reason: Refactored beeatlas.rkt placement to single-source lists: sandbox-marts (9 dbt outputs), placed-marts (6 copied), export-dir-files (terminal single-file exports), raw-file-paths. Derived from them: the 9 mart 'file artifacts, dbt-build #:outputs, mart-export-artifacts, place-marts inputs/outputs, place-marts-script, and beeatlas-path resolution. Collapsed beeatlas-path/case (sandbox case-list + terminal enum) into one cond over the lists. Removed the mart-files duplication. Directory outputs (species-maps/place-maps/feeds) deliberately kept unresolvable (#f) so the st-6qc guard still refuses them — placement-as-property did NOT auto-derive export-dir for all file outputs (that would defeat st-6qc and mismodel dir outputs). Behavior-preserving: 224 tests pass, edge-verify 4/4 clean, places.json/occurrences.db plans resolve unchanged.

title: --verify harness: derive out-file from target + handle post-dbt suffix inputs
description: verify-determinism (src/determinism.rkt) hardcodes out-file default 'occurrences.db' and main.rkt never passes the target as out-file, so --verify only meaningfully works for occurrences.db. Two gaps for post-dbt targets like species.json/collectors.json/places.json: (1) out-file should be derived from the target artifact's on-disk name (beeatlas-path); (2) --verify --from <suffix> builds into a fresh temp dir that lacks the upstream @export inputs (place-marts copies, species.parquet@export), so a suffix build fails with FileNotFoundError. Either build the full plan for post-dbt targets, or seed the clean dir with upstream outputs. Surfaced while building+verifying places.json (st-4cm slice 3); determinism there was instead evidenced by byte-identical direct-invocation parity + deterministic cache-skip.
close_reason: --verify now works for post-dbt terminals. (1) out-file derived from the target artifact's on-disk name via beeatlas-path (was hardcoded occurrences.db); (2) a --from suffix's fresh build dirs are seeded with the suffix's EXTERNAL @export inputs (verify-seeds in main.rkt) from the populated scratch dir, so the suffix builds without the upstream cone. determinism.rkt gained #:seed + #:out-file wiring. Demonstrated DETERMINISTIC on counties/ecoregions.clean.geojson, collectors.events.json, and the 16MB collector_event_pages.json sidecar. Bonus: the st-6qc guard (run by --verify) surfaced a latent slice-4 gap — collector_event_pages.json was missing from export-dir-files (unresolvable); fixed, and plan-test now guards both terminals' full output resolvability. 245 tests green.

title: place-marts node: place dbt marts in EXPORT_DIR; wire collectors.json (st-4cm slice 2)
description: st-4cm slice 2. Post-dbt exports read the dbt marts from EXPORT_DIR/*.parquet, but Stelis's dbt-build recipe (run.sh build only) omits run.py's _run_dbt_build copy of 6 marts (occurrences.parquet, occurrence_places.parquet, counties/ecoregions/wilderness.geojson, checklist.parquet) sandbox->EXPORT_DIR. Model that placement as an explicit 'place-marts' node (st-d44.4 explicit output destinations) producing @export artifacts, leaving the occurrences.db path (sandbox-direct) untouched. Then wire+verify collectors.json (reads EXPORT_DIR occurrences.parquet from place-marts + species.parquet from species-export).
